Party_Details.Name: Living the Link Pte Ltd (in creditors’ voluntary liquidation)

Facts.Text: Living the Link Pte Ltd was incorporated to carry on a new enterprise.
Living entered into a tenancy agreement for One Nassim with Cheong.
Living closed the business and terminated the lease.
Living was placed in creditors’ voluntary liquidation.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the transfers of inventory and shares by Living the Link Pte Ltd to its associate companies were influenced by a desire to prefer these companies over other creditors.
Party_Details.Rule: A transaction is an undue preference if it is influenced by a desire to place a creditor in a better position in the event of insolvency.
Party_Details.Application: The liquidators argued that the transfers were made when Living was insolvent and were influenced by a desire to prefer the associate companies. The defendants claimed the transfers were made in the ordinary course of business.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the transfers of inventory and shares were undue preferences as they were influenced by a desire to prefer the associate companies over other creditors.

Party_Details.Name: Tan Lay Tin Tina

Facts.Text: Tina Tan was a director and the sole shareholder of Living the Link Pte Ltd.
Tina Tan procured the transfer of inventory from Living to associate companies.
Tina Tan procured the transfer of Graha shares from Living to Link.
Tina Tan was aware of Living's financial difficulties and cash-flow issues.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director is legally responsible for procuring transactions that constitute undue preferences.
Party_Details.Rule: Directors have a duty to act in the best interest of the company and its creditors when the company is insolvent.
Party_Details.Application: The liquidators argued that Tina Tan contravened her duties by preferring the associate companies. Tina Tan claimed she acted in the best interest of the company.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court held that Tina Tan breached her duties as a director by procuring undue preferences, influenced by a desire to prefer the associate companies.
